capacity_basis: double occupancy (HAL Fleet Specifications 'Capacity' row); 'Maximum Passengers' full-occupancy figure recorded in each ship's notes

ships.name: Koningsdam
features: World Stage two-deck theater with production shows
Music Walk: Rolling Stone Lounge, Billboard Onboard, B.B. King's Blues Club
Rudi's Sel de Mer and Tamarind specialty dining
Lido Pool with retractable glass roof and adults-only Sea View Pool
ships.notes: Maiden voyage Apr 8, 2016. Maximum Passengers 3,154. Crew 1,036. First Pinnacle-class ship.

exp.overview: The lead ship of Holland America's flagship Pinnacle Class, christened by Queen Máxima of the Netherlands, Koningsdam pairs a music-and-design theme with the line's most ambitious dining and entertainment line-up. Named decks honour composers — Beethoven, Gershwin, Mozart, Schubert — and the ship centres on the two-story World Stage theatre and the multi-venue Music Walk. A 2026 dry dock refreshed the live-music spaces.

dining.name: Dining Room
dining.desc: The elegant two-deck main Dining Room serving rotating multi-course menus with both fixed and open seating.

dining.name: Lido Market
dining.desc: Bright marketplace-style buffet on the Lido Deck with made-to-order and grab-and-go stations.

dining.name: Pinnacle Grill
dining.desc: The line's signature steak-and-seafood restaurant, the fleet's flagship specialty room.

dining.name: Tamarind
dining.desc: Pan-Asian dining drawing on the flavours of Southeast Asia, China and Japan, paired with the Nami sushi bar.

dining.name: Canaletto
dining.desc: Relaxed Italian restaurant tucked into the Lido, serving shareable classics family-style.

dining.name: Rudi's Sel de Mer
dining.desc: A French-style seafood brasserie created with master chef Rudi Sodamin.

dining.name: Dive-In
dining.desc: Poolside grill turning out made-to-order gourmet burgers and hot dogs by the Lido Pool.

dining.name: New York Deli & Pizza
dining.desc: Casual spot for New York-style pizza, sandwiches and deli fare.

dining.name: Grand Dutch Cafe
dining.desc: A cosy Dutch cafe pouring specialty coffees and Netherlands-inspired bites and beers in the atrium.

activities.name: Greenhouse Spa & Salon
activities.desc: Full-service spa and salon with a thermal suite, hydro-pool and treatment menu.

activities.name: Lido Pool
activities.desc: Central pool sheltered under a retractable glass dome for all-weather swimming.

activities.name: Sea View Pool
activities.desc: Aft pool and terrace open to the ship's wake for adults-oriented relaxation.

activities.name: Culinary programming
activities.desc: Hands-on cooking demonstrations and tastings through Holland America's culinary and EXC enrichment program.

activities.name: Pickleball
activities.desc: Ocean-view sport court — part of the fleetwide pickleball roll-out.

activities.name: Fitness Center
activities.desc: Ocean-view gym with cardio, strength equipment and group classes.

entertainment.name: World Stage
entertainment.desc: The two-story main theatre wrapped in a panoramic 270-degree LED screen for immersive shows.

entertainment.name: Rolling Stone Rock Room
entertainment.desc: A live band plays a Rolling Stone-curated songbook of rock classics (rebranded Rolling Stone Lounge in the 2026 refresh).

entertainment.name: Billboard Onboard
entertainment.desc: Dueling pianos running through chart hits across the decades.

entertainment.name: B.B. King's Blues Club
entertainment.desc: A Memphis-style band brings soul, blues and R&B to the Music Walk late into the night.

exp.kids_family: Club HAL (branded Kids Club) offers supervised, age-tiered activities for ages 3–12 with a separate tween/teen space and a 'High Score!' arcade. Programming runs on select sailings and is lighter on adult-oriented itineraries.
exp.who_for: Aimed squarely at Holland America's core: mature, well-travelled guests who choose a premium mid-size ship for its culinary depth and live-music program over waterslides and crowds. A strong fit for couples, music lovers and multi-generational family groups who still want a refined, unhurried atmosphere.
exp.deploy_note: Officially listed on Holland America's Alaska program (summer, roundtrip ex-Seattle and Vancouver). Typically repositions to Mexican Riviera / Pacific ex-San Diego in winter — verify current season.

ships.name: Nieuw Statendam
features: World Stage productions with LED stagecraft
Rolling Stone Lounge, Billboard Onboard, B.B. King's Blues Club
Morimoto By Sea, Sel de Mer and Tamarind specialty dining
Grand Dutch Cafe and Club Orange private dining
ships.notes: Maiden voyage Dec 5, 2018. Maximum Passengers 3,170. Crew 1,036.

exp.overview: The second Pinnacle-Class ship, Nieuw Statendam carries the same music-inspired design language as Koningsdam with a slightly enlarged layout and an added Sky Deck. Its heart is the two-story World Stage with a wraparound LED screen, surrounded by the Music Walk's cluster of live-music venues, and it houses Holland America's full specialty-dining roster.

entertainment.name: World Stage

entertainment.name: Rolling Stone Rock Room
entertainment.desc: Live band performing a Rolling Stone-curated rock songbook (rebranded Rolling Stone Lounge in the fleet refresh).

entertainment.name: Billboard Onboard
entertainment.desc: Dueling pianos powering through chart-topping hits.

entertainment.name: B.B. King's Blues Club
entertainment.desc: A Memphis-style band delivering blues, soul and R&B in the Music Walk.

exp.kids_family: Club HAL (Kids Club) provides age-tiered supervised activities for ages 3–12, a tween/teen space and a 'High Score!' game room; offered on select sailings.
exp.who_for: Built for Holland America's mature, premium-leaning guest who prizes an elevated culinary scene and nightly live music. Ideal for couples and small groups seeking a polished, sophisticated ship rather than a resort-at-sea aimed at kids.
exp.deploy_note: Typically Caribbean and Panama Canal out of Fort Lauderdale in winter and Europe/Mediterranean in summer; not on the current US/Canada Alaska list. Verify specific seasons and home ports.

ships.name: Rotterdam
features: World Stage theater with dynamic production shows
Rolling Stone Lounge, Billboard Onboard, B.B. King's Blues Club
Morimoto By Sea, Sel de Mer, Tamarind and Club Orange dining
Grand Dutch Cafe and Half Moon Bar
ships.notes: Maiden voyage August 1, 2021 (largest ship in the fleet by tonnage). Maximum Passengers 3,172. Crew 1,048.

exp.overview: The newest Pinnacle-Class ship and the seventh to carry Holland America's cornerstone name, Rotterdam is the line's namesake flagship. It showcases a museum-quality art collection valued at more than four million dollars, the two-story World Stage with 270-degree LED projection, and the unique Half Moon bar telling the line's history through cocktails. A 2026 dry dock added a Crow's Nest performance stage and a Sea View Pool bandstand.

entertainment.name: B.B. King's Blues Club

exp.kids_family: Club HAL (Kids Club) with age-tiered supervised activities for ages 3–12 and a tween/teen space; offered on select sailings.
exp.who_for: The choice for Holland America loyalists and discerning, culturally-minded travellers who want the line's flagship experience — deep art and history touches, top-tier dining and the richest live-music program at sea. Best suited to couples and mature guests.
exp.deploy_note: Typically Caribbean out of Fort Lauderdale in winter and Europe in summer; a 2026 dry dock kept it current. Not on the official Alaska list. Verify home ports and season.

ships.name: Eurodam
features: World Stage productions
Rolling Stone Lounge, Billboard Onboard and The Dam Band
Pinnacle Grill and Tamarind pan-Asian dining
Lido Pool with retractable glass roof, adults-only Sea View Pool, Retreat Cabanas
ships.notes: Maiden voyage Jul 1, 2008. Maximum Passengers 2,731. Crew 876. First Signature-class ship.

exp.overview: The first Signature-Class ship, Eurodam is a comfortable mid-size vessel that packs in Holland America's music-and-dining hallmarks a step down in scale from the Pinnacle ships. It features the two-story World Stage, a Music Walk with live rock and dueling pianos, the Pan-Asian Tamarind restaurant, and a retractable-dome Lido Pool alongside an adults-only Sea View Pool.

exp.overview: The second Signature-Class ship, Nieuw Amsterdam wears an Art Deco, New York City-inspired design with a museum-quality art collection. It offers the two-story World Stage, a Music Walk of live rock and dueling pianos, the Pan-Asian Tamarind, an ocean-view pickleball court, and a retractable-dome Lido Pool paired with an adults-only Sea View Pool.

ships.notes: Maiden voyage Feb 22, 2006. Maximum Passengers 2,596. Crew 811.

exp.overview: A Vista-Class ship, Noordam is a mid-size vessel centred on a three-story atrium and known for its art collection. It carries the two-story World Stage, a Music Walk with live rock and dueling pianos, an ocean-view pickleball court and a therapeutic hydro-pool, plus a retractable-dome Lido Pool and adults-only Sea View Pool.

exp.overview: A Vista-Class ship, Westerdam completed a January 2026 dry dock that redesigned its Crow's Nest observation lounge with a new live-music bandstand and refreshed the atrium, Canaletto, Lido Market and Dive-In. It offers the two-story World Stage, a live rock room, and a retractable-dome Lido Pool with an adults-only Sea View Pool.

ships.name: Volendam
features: Wajang Theater productions and cinematic screenings
Pinnacle Grill, Canaletto and Morimoto By Sea
MIX lounge, Piano Bar and Library Cafe
Lanai staterooms (ocean-view with direct promenade access)
ships.notes: Maiden voyage Nov 12, 1999. Maximum Passengers 1,839. Crew 604. R-class; uses Wajang Theater rather than World Stage. 21 Lanai staterooms per Fleet Specifications.

exp.overview: An R-Class ship and one of the two smallest, most intimate vessels in the fleet, Volendam spreads across ten decks with a floral design theme. It centres on the World Stage show lounge and the distinctive Wajang Theater cinema — an R-Class signature — rather than a full Music Walk, and offers Pinnacle Grill and Canaletto specialty dining, the Greenhouse Spa, and both Lido and Sea View pools.

dining.desc: The line's signature steak-and-seafood restaurant.

dining.name: Canaletto
dining.desc: Italian dining within the Lido, served family-style.

dining.name: Dive-In
dining.desc: Poolside grill for gourmet burgers and hot dogs by the Lido Pool.

activities.name: Greenhouse Spa & Salon
activities.desc: Spa and salon with treatment rooms, thermal features and a fitness centre.

activities.name: Lido Pool
activities.desc: Central Lido Deck pool and lounging area.

activities.name: Sea View Pool
activities.desc: Aft pool open to the sea.

activities.name: Culinary programming
activities.desc: Cooking demonstrations and EXC enrichment sessions.

activities.name: Fitness Center
activities.desc: Ocean-view gym with cardio and strength equipment.

entertainment.name: World Stage
entertainment.desc: The main show lounge for the ship's production shows and headline entertainment.

entertainment.name: Wajang Theater
entertainment.desc: A dedicated cinema for films and screenings — a distinctive R-Class feature not found on the larger ships.

exp.kids_family: Club HAL (Kids Club) with supervised activities for ages 3–12; offered on select sailings and lighter on this intimate, adult-leaning ship.
exp.who_for: Ideal for mature guests who prefer a small, intimate, classic ship over a big-ship experience — well suited to couples and enrichment-minded travellers drawn to longer, destination-rich itineraries. Less oriented to families and the elaborate live-music program of the larger classes.
exp.deploy_note: Typically longer voyages including Caribbean, Panama Canal, Canada/New England and grand voyages; not on the current Alaska list. Verify home ports and season.

ships.name: Zaandam
features: Wajang Theater productions and daily movies
Pinnacle Grill, Canaletto and Morimoto By Sea
MIX martini & champagne lounge and Piano Bar
Library Cafe and Thermal Suite
ships.notes: Maiden voyage May 6, 2000. Maximum Passengers 1,839. Crew 604. R-class; uses Wajang Theater rather than World Stage.

exp.overview: An R-Class ship and sister to Volendam, Zaandam is a small, intimate ten-deck vessel with a music-inspired decor theme. Like its sister it is built around the World Stage show lounge and the Wajang Theater cinema rather than a full Music Walk, and offers Pinnacle Grill and Canaletto specialty dining, the Greenhouse Spa, and both Lido and Sea View pools.

dining.name: Dining Room
dining.desc: The two-deck main Dining Room with rotating multi-course menus.

dining.name: Lido Market
dining.desc: Marketplace-style buffet with cooked-to-order and grab-and-go stations.

dining.name: Pinnacle Grill
dining.desc: The line's signature steak-and-seafood restaurant.

dining.name: Canaletto
dining.desc: Italian dining within the Lido, served family-style.

dining.name: Dive-In
dining.desc: Poolside grill for gourmet burgers and hot dogs by the Lido Pool.

entertainment.name: World Stage

entertainment.name: Wajang Theater
entertainment.desc: A dedicated cinema for films and screenings — a distinctive R-Class feature.

exp.kids_family: Club HAL (Kids Club) with supervised activities for ages 3–12; offered on select sailings and lighter on this intimate, adult-leaning ship.
exp.who_for: Best for mature travellers who want a small, classic, intimate ship for destination-focused and Alaska voyages — well suited to couples and enrichment-minded guests rather than families seeking big-ship amenities.
exp.deploy_note: Officially listed on Holland America's Alaska program (summer, including one-way Gulf of Alaska sailings ex-Vancouver/Whittier). Other seasons typically Pacific Coastal, Mexico and Canada/New England — verify.

flag: All 11 ships have officially-sourced class, year, tonnage and guests. IMPORTANT: the official fleet-page 'Ships at a Glance' PDF (/content/dam/hal/inventory-assets/ships/at-a-glance/hal-ships-at-a-glance.pdf, updated 11/27/25) is a DINING/AMENITIES/ENTERTAINMENT comparison chart ONLY and contains NO tonnage/guests/year. Specs were instead taken from the official Holland America Line Fleet Specifications fact sheet PDF (newsroom press kit, effective date Nov 17, 2020) - authoritative and first-party, but ~5 years old; capacity/tonnage/year are stable but a fresh official fact sheet should be re-checked before publish. Features were read from each ship's official hollandamerica.com/en/us/cruise-ships/<ship> detail page. No prices recorded. No third-party sources used.
